concepts.title: Kähler differentials $\Omega^1_{X/k}$
concepts.blurb: $\Omega^1_{X/k}$ is the universal recipient of $k$-derivations — algebraic 1-forms on $X$. For $X$ smooth of dimension $n$, $\Omega^1_{X/k}$ is locally free of rank $n$ and $\Omega^p_{X/k} := \bigwedge^p \Omega^1_{X/k}$ encodes algebraic $p$-forms.

concepts.title: The algebraic de Rham complex $\Omega^\bullet_{X/k}$
concepts.blurb: The complex $\Omega^0_{X/k} \xrightarrow{d} \Omega^1_{X/k} \xrightarrow{d} \Omega^2_{X/k} \to \cdots$ has differential given by exterior derivative. Its hypercohomology is the algebraic de Rham cohomology $H^*_{dR}(X/k)$ — a purely algebraic invariant.

concepts.title: Comparison with Betti cohomology
concepts.blurb: For a smooth complex variety $X$, Grothendieck's comparison theorem identifies $H^*_{dR}(X/\mathbb{C})$ with the singular cohomology $H^*(X(\mathbb{C}), \mathbb{C})$. The algebraic side computes the same invariant — without any analytic input.

concepts.title: The Hodge filtration $F^\bullet$
concepts.blurb: $F^p H^n_{dR}(X) = \mathrm{im}\,H^n(\sigma^{\ge p} \Omega^\bullet_{X/k})$ is the decreasing filtration coming from the trivial truncation of the de Rham complex. For $X$ smooth projective over $\mathbb{C}$, the associated graded is $\bigoplus_p H^{n-p}(X, \Omega^p_X)$ — the Hodge decomposition.

concepts.title: Hodge numbers $h^{p,q}(X)$
concepts.blurb: $h^{p,q}(X) = \dim H^q(X, \Omega^p_X)$ is a sheaf-cohomology dimension — but for $X$ smooth projective over $\mathbb{C}$ they assemble into a Hodge diamond reflecting Serre duality and complex conjugation symmetry. Topological data refining the Betti numbers.

concepts.title: Worked example: de Rham of a smooth curve
concepts.blurb: For a smooth projective curve $C$ of genus $g$ over $\mathbb{C}$: $H^0_{dR}(C) = \mathbb{C}$, $H^2_{dR}(C) = \mathbb{C}$ (canonical class), and $H^1_{dR}(C)$ has dimension $2g$ split as $H^0(C, \Omega^1) \oplus H^1(C, \mathcal{O})$ by the Hodge filtration.
